mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding supply-side output that lifts corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are not positive in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicate a stagflationary monetary environment where rising inflation coincides with tightening policy, compressing profit margins and valuation multiples to generate negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of joint-occurrence months over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime where UNRATE exceeds 7%, month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal a labor market recovery that boosts consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month PAYEMS increases when UNRATE exceeds 7% fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal strengthening residential investment and construction activity, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through cyclical sector earnings expansion.
failure_condition: If positive month-over-month changes in HOUST are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 58% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 2% signals an emerging supply-side recession that reduces corporate profitability and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO contractions exceeding 2% over 3 months do not precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening of financial conditions that raises discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Joint quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) and month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) confirm a broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate revenues across sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If simultaneous quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth and month-over-month RSAFS growth fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of joint-occurrence periods over any 3-year rolling window, reject.
